entries.intro: Someone's been helping themselves to eggs around here. Three mornings in a row now. Coop's half empty.
details: No broken shells, no feathers. Just fewer eggs than there should be.
I asked around and a couple of other folks said the same thing. So it's not just me.
reactions: Could be a fox, could be a kid with quick hands. Either way, I'm annoyed.

statCheck.pass: No tracks around the coop and no damage to the wire. That rules out most animals. Probably a snake getting in through the gap underneath.
statCheck.fail: Beats me who's doing it. Eggs don't just walk off on their own.

entries.intro: A good saw went missing from my shed. Left it leaning against the wall and it was gone by morning.
details: I've had that saw for years. It wasn't fancy but it was mine.
I asked a few people if they'd borrowed it. Everyone said no. Very helpful.
reactions: I'm more irritated than anything. Now I have to get a new one and they're not cheap.

entries.intro: Food's been going missing from the storehouse. Just a little at a time. A sack of grain here, some dried meat there.
details: Nobody noticed at first. It's the kind of thing you only catch when you do a proper count.
The lock's not broken and there's no sign of a mess. Whoever it is, they're tidy about it.
reactions: It's not a disaster, but it adds up. People are starting to keep closer count of their own stores.

statCheck.pass: Small amounts taken neatly. That's usually someone desperate, not greedy. Taking just enough to get by.
statCheck.fail: I've got no idea who it is. Could be anyone.

entries.intro: Some rope and a few stakes went missing the other day. Not valuable stuff. That's what's odd about it.
details: Who steals rope? You can buy it for next to nothing. It's more puzzling than anything.
A couple of buckets disappeared around the same time. Someone's setting something up out there.
reactions: Honestly I'm more curious than upset. It's such a strange assortment of things.

entries.intro: That rake I thought I lost? Found it behind the woodpile. I must have put it there myself and forgot.
details: I spent two days blaming the wind. Turns out I'm just getting forgetful.
My partner had a good laugh about it. Said I'd lose my own head if it wasn't attached.
reactions: Maybe half the things that go missing just end up behind a woodpile somewhere.
